title: Partition
statement: Let $a<b$ and set $I=[a,b]$. A finite ordered set $P=\{x_0,\ldots,x_n\}$ with $n\in\mathbb{N}$ and $n\ge 1$ is a partition of $I$ if and only if $x_0=a$, $x_n=b$, and $x_0<x_1<\cdots<x_n$. In this case the closed subintervals $[x_{i-1},x_i]$ for $i=1,\ldots,n$ subdivide $I$.
